data.title: Buy Rating on Compass Therapeutics: Anticipating Pivotal Tovecimig Data as a Key Value Inflection Point in Advanced Biliary Tract Cancer
data.description:
William Blair analyst Matt Phipps has maintained their bullish stance on CMPX stock, giving a Buy rating on March 26.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Matt Phipps has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors surrounding Compass Therapeutics’ lead asset, tovecimig, in biliary tract cancer. He anticipates pivotal Phase II/III data imminently, with a particular focus on progression-free and overall survival, and views the upcoming results as a key value inflection point given the high unmet need in advanced BTC.

He also underscores that the current treatment options for advanced BTC remain limited, and a positive outcome versus paclitaxel in the comparator arm could meaningfully shift the treatment paradigm. In his view, the company’s engagement with leading experts like Dr. Lipika Goyal and its positioning within the broader BTC development landscape support the potential for clinical and commercial upside, justifying a Buy recommendation on CMPX.

In another report released on March 26, H.C. Wainwright also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $24.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Peabody Energy: Centurion Setback Creates Near-Term Pressure but Supports Undervalued Long-Term Upside
data.description:
Chris LaFemina, an analyst from Jefferies, assigned the Buy rating on Peabody Energy Comm. The associated price target was raised to $44.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Chris LaFemina has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that, in his view, still support upside in BTU despite the Centurion setback. He acknowledges near-term pressure from lower-than-expected first quarter metallurgical coal shipments caused by commissioning and equipment issues at the Centurion mine, which have led him to reduce earnings forecasts and trim volume expectations for 2026 and slightly for 2027.

However, he emphasizes that equipment problems appear to be addressed, the full-year met coal shipment guidance has been maintained, and Centurion remains central to Peabody’s strategy as a future source of substantial low-cost premium met coal volumes. Combined with robust seaborne thermal coal pricing, he believes these factors should drive meaningful free cash flow and earnings growth, leaving BTU trading on what he views as undemanding 2027 valuation metrics and justifying a continued Buy recommendation.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Palo Alto Networks: CEO Share Purchase and AI-Driven Demand Reinforce Durable Security Moat and Buy Rating
data.description:
Robert W. Baird analyst Shrenik Kothari maintained a Buy rating on Palo Alto Networks today and set a price target of $220.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shrenik Kothari has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that collectively underscore strengthening fundamentals and competitive positioning at Palo Alto Networks. He views the CEO’s roughly $10M open-market share purchase as a powerful, high-conviction signal that management believes current market concerns are overdone and that the long-term opportunity for the platform remains underappreciated.

Kothari also emphasizes that rising AI and agentic workflows actually deepen, rather than diminish, the need for PANW’s capabilities, as enterprises must enforce identity-driven, real-time security controls at scale. In his assessment, PANW’s role in runtime monitoring, policy enforcement, and large-scale telemetry collection — including blocking tens of billions of attacks per day — reinforces a durable moat that should support growth and justify a positive stance on the stock.

In another report released today, Barclays also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $200.00 price target.

data.title: Genpact: Buy Rated on Strengthening AI-Enabled Growth, Higher-Margin Revenue, and Outcome-Based Scalability
data.description:
Analyst Maggie Nolan of William Blair maintained a Buy rating on Genpact, retaining the price target of $37.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Maggie Nolan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that underscore Genpact’s improving growth profile and strategic positioning in AI‑enabled services. She views the company’s advanced technology solutions as a key engine of higher‑margin, annuity‑like revenue, with strong data and AI pipeline momentum expected to support sustained double‑digit expansion.

At the same time, Nolan highlights the steady evolution of Genpact’s legacy business and contract structures toward outcome‑based and transaction‑linked models that should lift profitability and scalability over time. Her conviction is reinforced by management’s clear execution roadmap, focus on client adoption of agentic solutions, and confidence in meeting medium‑term financial objectives laid out for investors.

data.title: Dauch Corporation: GM-Driven Upside Offset by Macro Headwinds Supports Neutral Hold at $8 Target
data.description:
Stifel Nicolaus analyst Nathan Jones has maintained their neutral stance on DCH stock, giving a Hold rating today.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Nathan Jones has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors related to both opportunity and risk for Dauch Corporation. On the positive side, Dauch stands to gain from General Motors ramping up production of its high-margin Silverado and Sierra trucks, where Dauch has strong content and GM accounts for roughly over a quarter of its sales, with additional upside from the company’s sizeable North American exposure as GM boosts U.S. output.

Balancing this, Jones underscores that macro headwinds limit conviction for a more bullish stance, notably the sharp rise in Brent crude following the conflict in the Middle East and the potential for sustained fuel prices to erode U.S. vehicle demand after several months. He also points out that industry-wide production forecasts remain essentially unchanged, suggesting limited broader volume tailwinds, which leads him to conclude that while Dauch has clear incremental benefits from GM’s actions, the overall risk‑reward profile justifies maintaining a neutral Hold with an $8 price target rather than upgrading the stock.

Jones covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as A. O. Smith Corporation, Parker Hannifin, and Curtiss-Wright. According to TipRanks, Jones has an average return of 20.9% and a 69.57% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, TipRanks – xAI also reiterated a Hold rating on the stock with a $6.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
